steps.title: Identify the rhetorical focus
steps.content: The question asks which choice emphasizes “when”—and the notes provide the specific timing: 1996. The key signal is that effective emphasis requires putting the temporal detail in the foreground, not burying it in the explanation.

steps.title: Match to choices
steps.content: Choice D inverts the sentence structure—”It was in 1996 that...”—which places 1996 at the front as the focal point. The others either omit the year (A, C) or foreground the reason for the award instead (B). That matches choice D — D is correct.
